title: Verilator with --binary on MacOs
body:
Hello!

Hope you're doing well. I've had to resort to working on my MacBook recently. On my primary system, I've been taking full advantage of the new `--binary` feature (great feature btw) which works great on linux. On my M2 MacBook, I'm not sure exactly what is going on, but it seems that at some point during verilation, the tool will  run a file that has an ```std::coroutine_handle``` that clang doesnt seem to provide out of the box, at least not without a flag of some sort. The complete error is: 

```
 ~/Repos/test/ c++ -Os -I.  -MMD -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/verilator/5.026/share/verilator/include -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/verilator/5.026/share/verilator/include/vltstd -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TRACE=0 -DVM_TRACE_FST=0 -DVM_TRACE_VCD=0 -faligned-new -fbracket-depth=4096 -fcf-protection=none -Qunused-arguments -Wno-bool-operation -Wno-c++11-narrowing -Wno-constant-logical-operand -Wno-non-pod-varargs -Wno-parentheses-equality -Wno-shadow -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-tautological-bitwise-compare -Wno-tautological-compare -Wno-uninitialized -Wno-unused-but-set-parameter -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-variable    -DVL_TIME_CONTEXT  -std=gnu++17 -fcoroutines-ts -c -o verilated_timing.o /opt/homebrew/Cellar/verilator/5.026/share/verilator/include/verilated_timing.cpp
In file included from /opt/homebrew/Cellar/verilator/5.026/share/verilator/include/verilated_timing.cpp:23:
/opt/homebrew/Cellar/verilator/5.026/share/verilator/include/verilated_timing.h:104:10: error: no template named 'coroutine_handle' in namespace 'std'
    std::coroutine_handle<> m_coro;  // The wrapped coroutine handle
    ~~~~~^
/opt/homebrew/Cellar/verilator/5.026/share/verilator/include/verilated_timing.h:118:28: error: no template named 'coroutine_handle' in namespace 'std'
    VlCoroutineHandle(std::coroutine_handle<> coro, VlProcessRef process, VlFileLineDebug fileline)
                      ~~~~~^
/opt/homebrew/Cellar/verilator/5.026/share/verilator/include/verilated_timing.h:208:37: error: no template named 'coroutine_handle' in namespace 'std'
            void await_suspend(std::coroutine_handle<> coro) {
```

I went ahead and performed a few sanity checks to make sure I wasn't using a broken install of verilator or something. Running a simple testbench the old school way has no problems, both for a simple counter design and my actual much larger design. I also built a simple coroutine hello world program using std::couroutine_handle and had no problems running with g++ -std=c++20.

I also tried forcing verilator to use g++ (actual g++, not a g++ call that just links to clang) when verilating, but that was unfortunately not very helpful. I'm not exactly sure what else to try. If anyone has suggestions, please do let me know. Thanks in advance.
